title: Trump invited the Artemis 2 moon astronauts to the Oval Office. Here's what happened
summary: President Trump met with the Artemis 2 moon astronauts at the White House, congratulating them and expressing interest in space travel. The event highlighted U.S. efforts to return humans to the moon under the Artemis program.

title: Is Ben Mauro's 'Huxley' graphic novel universe the next big thing in sci-fi? (interview)
summary: Ben Mauro's 'Huxley' graphic novel universe is gaining attention as a potential major player in sci-fi. Mauro expresses excitement about its growth and expansion.

title: James Webb Space Telescope's strange little red dots may really be 'black hole stars', X-ray data suggests
summary: X-ray data from the James Webb Space Telescope's observations of 'little red dots' suggests they may be 'black hole stars'. These objects could help answer what these mysterious entities truly are.

title: Europe's powerful Ariane 6 rocket launching 32 Amazon internet satellites early April 30: Watch it live
summary: Europe's Ariane 6 rocket is set to launch 32 Amazon internet satellites on April 30, with the event available to watch live. This marks a significant commercial mission for the newly operational heavy-lift rocket.

title: 'Star Trek: Strange New Worlds' season 4 gets release date and trailer packed with space cowboys, black holes, and a T-rex
summary: Star Trek: Strange New Worlds season 4 has been announced with a release date and a trailer featuring space cowboys, black holes, and a T-rex. The show's producers claim it is their best season yet.

title: The cosmos wears a galactic sombrero | Space photo of the day for April 29, 2026
summary: A space photo of the Sombrero galaxy was featured as the Space Photo of the Day on April 29, 2026. The galaxy's distinctive shape resembles a galactic sombrero.

title: SpaceX rocket debris could slam into the moon: Here's what you need to know
summary: A spent SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket stage from a 2022 launch is expected to collide with the moon in early August. The debris is predicted to impact the lunar surface, raising concerns about potential contamination.

title: Help scientists find spacetime warps in these Euclid Space Telescope images
summary: A new citizen science project invites the public to analyze Euclid Space Telescope images to identify galaxies causing spacetime warps. This initiative aims to engage the public in cutting-edge astronomical research.

title: What is quantum gravity? Scientists think it could explain the beginning of our universe
summary: Scientists propose a new 'quadratic gravity' model to better understand quantum gravity and its implications for the Big Bang and the singularity before the universe's beginning.

title: Is Tatooine the norm? Planets may prefer living with two suns instead of one
summary: New simulations suggest that binary star systems might be more conducive to planet formation than single-star systems, potentially producing more gas giants. This challenges the traditional view that single-star systems like our own are the norm for planetary formation.

title: I Am Artemis: Ryan Schulte
summary: NASA's Orion spacecraft on the Artemis II mission included a flywheel exercise device to maintain astronaut health during a 694,481-mile journey around the Moon. Ryan Schulte, Orion flywheel project manager, discussed the device's role in supporting the crew's physical and mental well-being.

title: US-Indian Spacecraft Captures Mexico City Subsidence
summary: NASA-ISRO's NISAR mission captured data showing land subsidence in Mexico City between October 2025 and January 2026. The mission's performance is validated by these high-resolution images of a known subsidence hotspot.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4873-4878: Welcome to the Atacama Drill Target
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover began a new drill campaign at the Atacama site on Mars to study layered-sulfate bedrock, marking its first such investigation since leaving the boxwork terrain. The mission, led by the Curiosity Science Team, aims to characterize geological features to understand the planet's past environment.

title: US-Indian Space Mission Maps Extreme Subsidence in Mexico City
summary: The NISAR satellite, a joint NASA-ISRO mission, mapped extreme subsidence in Mexico City using advanced radar technology. The findings demonstrate the satellite's ability to track real-time surface changes without interference from clouds or vegetation.

title: A Gently Glowing Galaxy
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the barred spiral galaxy IC 486, located 380 million light-years from Earth in the constellation Gemini. The galaxy's luminous, ethereal glow is highlighted in the image taken on April 13, 2026.

title: NASA, Boeing Advance Truss-Braced Wing Research in Test
summary: NASA and Boeing have completed wind tunnel testing for a truss-braced wing aircraft design aimed at improving aerodynamic efficiency. The innovative configuration could reduce fuel and operational costs for future airliners.

title: NASA Demonstrates New Prescribed Burn Capability for Spaceport
summary: NASA demonstrated a new prescribed burn capability at Kennedy Space Center in January 2026, aimed at enhancing safety and environmental management during launch operations. The initiative highlights NASA's focus on reducing unintended smoke and fire risks beyond rocket launches.

title: Council on Environmental Quality Permitting Innovators Program
summary: NASA's Center of Excellence for Collaborative Innovation (CoECI) assists the Council on Environmental Quality (CEQ) in using crowdsourcing for federal initiatives. CoECI's NASA Tournament Lab provides contract capabilities for running external crowdsourced challenges on behalf of NASA and other agencies.

title: NASA Connects Little Red Dots with Chandra, Webb
summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory and James Webb Space Telescope have identified a new object that may help explain mysterious 'little red dots' found in the early universe. This discovery could unlock the true nature of hundreds or thousands of similar objects detected in recent years.

title: SpaceX launches Falcon Heavy on Wednesday after 18 month hiatus
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Falcon Heavy rocket after an 18-month hiatus. This marks a significant return for the heavy-lift vehicle, which had not flown since June 2019.

title: Artemis III core stage assembly arrives at the Kennedy Space Center
summary: The core stage assembly for NASA's Artemis III mission has arrived at the Kennedy Space Center. This follows the recent Artemis II launch, which marked a historic milestone with four astronauts flying by the Moon.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon Heavy returns, Atlas V and Ariane 6 to launch Amazon Leo satellites
summary: Falcon Heavy, Atlas V, and Ariane 6 are set to launch Amazon's Leo satellites in six launches across Florida, California, and French Guiana. SpaceX's Falcon Heavy is returning for this mission.

title: Curiosity Rover uncovers fresh organic clues on Ancient Mars
summary: NASA's Curiosity Rover has discovered new organic compounds on Mars, providing fresh clues about the potential for ancient life on the planet. This finding marks a significant advancement in the search for extraterrestrial life.

title: Progress MS-34 to launch from Baikonur for ISS cargo delivery
summary: Progress MS-34 is scheduled to launch from Baikonur Cosmodrome to deliver cargo to the International Space Station (ISS), following the CRS NG-24 mission.

title: Starship Progress at the Cape with Hoping to Launch this year
summary: SpaceX is making progress on the Starship program at Cape Canaveral, aiming for a launch later this year as the first Block 3 flight approaches.

title: NASA and Axiom react to OIG Report on delays in Next-Generation Spacesuit Program
summary: NASA and Axiom Space have responded to a critical report from the NASA Office of Inspector General highlighting delays in the Next-Generation Spacesuit Program. The report raises concerns about the program's timeline and potential impacts on future missions.

title: Launch Preview: GPS, Progress, and Starlink missions to launch during busy week
summary: Seven space missions involving four countries and five launch vehicles are scheduled for the week of April 20, including GPS, Progress, and Starlink launches.

title: AST SpaceMobile’s BlueBird 7 declared lost following New Glenn launch
summary: AST SpaceMobile's BlueBird 7 satellite was declared lost after a failed launch aboard Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket. This marks a setback for the company's satellite broadband ambitions.

title: New Glenn launches on third mission, successfully reuses booster for the first time
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket successfully completed its third mission, marking the first reuse of its booster. The launch occurred just over five months after its second mission.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified space industry topic. No specific details about the content or focus of the news were provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements. The publication marks five decades of milestones in the European space sector.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: Researchers have developed a new electrostatic capture technology for safely docking spacecraft in orbit. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, confirming its readiness for a 2027 launch to search for Earth-like planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual consultation process, including an SME Forum in June 2026 at ESA Headquarters in Paris, with preparatory online sessions in April and May.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal was successfully received from an optimal location. This milestone marks a significant advancement in navigation technology.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission revealed that solar wind structures in the inner corona travel 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency’s technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology, likely focusing on improved precision or new methods for directional control. This innovation could enhance mission efficiency and accuracy in space operations.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated light-based propulsion using graphene aerogels during ESA's parabolic flight campaign in May 2025. The study, led by Université Libre de Bruxelles and Khalifa University, showed that lasers could propel the ultralight materials efficiently in microgravity conditions.

title: DPhi’s Second Demo Brings Compute Space to Orbit
summary: DPhi Space successfully demonstrated in-orbit AI processing by running a large language model on a satellite to analyze an Earth observation image without ground transmission. The experiment was part of DPhi's second mission, Clustergate-2, launched aboard Momentus' Vigoride 7 satellite in March.

title: Golden Dome Will Use COTS Parts, Space Execs Say
summary: Golden Dome satellite bus will utilize Commercial Off-The-Shelf (COTS) parts, according to Apex Space CEO Ian Cinnamon. The approach aims to standardize and simplify satellite manufacturing.

title: Dems Slam Trump Over NSF Board Cuts
summary: Democrats criticized former President Trump over proposed cuts to the National Science Foundation (NSF) board, calling it a detrimental move to science and innovation. The statement was made by Rep. Zoe Lofgren (D-CA) in response to the policy change.

title: Isaacman Continues to Defend Budget Cuts to Congress
summary: Isaacman defended budget cuts to Congress while emphasizing NASA's top goal of accelerating the US return to the Moon. The hearing highlighted the agency's priorities in space exploration.

title: True Anomaly Closes $650M Series D
summary: True Anomaly, a four-year-old space company, raised $650 million in a Series D funding round. The funding follows the company's recent Golden Dome award for its space-based interceptor technology.

title: Meta Secures Overview Energy Space Solar Power Capacity
summary: Meta has secured an agreement with Overview Energy to obtain up to 1 GW of power capacity through a planned solar power-beaming satellite system. This deal marks a significant step in leveraging space-based solar energy for large-scale applications.

title: Pentagon Taps 12 Companies for Golden Dome SBI Tech
summary: The Pentagon has selected 12 companies for the Golden Dome SBI Tech program, awarding contracts worth up to $3.2 billion combined. This initiative aims to advance space-based defense technologies.

title: Astra Targets Golden Dome With Small Rockets, Says CEO Chris Kemp
summary: Astra CEO Chris Kemp announced plans to use small rockets for target practice, aiming to reduce costs for commercial and government customers. The initiative is part of Astra's strategy to scale operations and improve efficiency.

title: Trump Taps Space Execs For Military Space Roles
summary: Donald Trump has appointed two space industry executives to military space roles, pending Senate confirmation. The nominees would take on positions within the U.S. military space sector if approved.

title: Astrobotic Hotfires Engine That Could Power Moon Missions
summary: Astrobotic successfully test-fired a Chakram engine prototype for five minutes at NASA’s Marshall Space Flight Center, potentially powering future Moon missions.

title: EarthDaily CEO Don Osborne Previews Upcoming Launch in Thursday Morning Conversation
summary: EarthDaily Analytics is preparing to launch six satellites this quarter, marking a significant milestone as it transitions to commercial service. CEO Don Osborne discussed the upcoming launch in a recent conversation.

title: Department of Air Force Plans Initiative to Hire AI Talent
summary: The U.S. Department of the Air Force (DAF) is launching an initiative to recruit top AI talent, which will impact the government's space strategy. This move aims to enhance technological capabilities in the space sector.

title: Canada Cancels WildFireSat Constellation Contract With Spire
summary: Canada has canceled its contract with Spire Global to develop the WildFireSat wildfire-monitoring satellite constellation. However, the government still intends to proceed with the space-based wildfire monitoring program.

title: SpaceX Launches Final ViaSat-3 Satellite, Completing Trio of GEO Satellites
summary: SpaceX successfully launched the final ViaSat-3 satellite, completing Viasat's trio of high-capacity Geostationary Orbit (GEO) satellites. The mission used a Falcon Heavy rocket from Kennedy Space Center.

title: Orbcomm Completes $460M Refinancing to Fund Growth
summary: Orbcomm has completed a $460 million refinancing to support growth initiatives, large-scale customer deployments, and platform expansion. The funding package was announced on Wednesday.

title: UPCOMING: Designing Secure Satellite Systems with FIPS 140-3, CNSA 2.0, and PQC
summary: Designing secure satellite systems is becoming challenging due to evolving security requirements like CNSA 2.0 and FIPS 140-3, which must remain secure for decades despite limited updates.

title: True Anomaly Raises $650 Million in New Round to Continue Scaling
summary: True Anomaly secured $650 million in a new funding round to expand its workforce and capabilities, aiming to meet growing customer demand.

title: ST Engineering Signs Singapore Public Safety Deal with HTX
summary: ST Engineering and HTX will co-develop space-based technologies to enhance Singapore's public safety operations through a new space technology program.

title: MaiaSpace CEO Says “Start Later, Run Faster” as Launcher Works Toward First Mission
summary: MaiaSpace is developing a new rocket for European launch services with a strategy to start later but operate faster. The company is working toward its first mission, aiming to disrupt industry trends.

title: Airbus Defence and Space Improves Revenue and Margin in Q1 2026
summary: Airbus Defence and Space reported improved revenue, adjusted EBIT, and order intake in Q1 2026, nearly doubling its order intake compared to the same period last year.
